_comment: Per-console saveSlot (the general (console, type) save-distinctness seam a link trade / critter-swap rides): two cabinets that would otherwise SHARE one battery save per cart type each keep their OWN by deriving a per-cabinet path ({base}.s{slot}.sav). Boot both, Cycle each to the same battery-backed game, and their high-score / progress saves no longer collide. This is the seam the overworld link cable drives over stdin: `Puck.Demo --run docs/examples/overworld-link-saves.json`, then pipe `boot 0` / `boot 1` / `link 0 1` / `press 0 a*4` / `press 1 a - a - a` — each cabinet holds its own save while the linked pair steps in lockstep and the diegetic cable glows while they exchange bytes. A null saveSlot (the default, and every other example) keeps the shared-per-type save, byte-unchanged.
